title: Date difference returns month_interval instead of day_interval
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE date_test (id INT PRIMARY KEY, start_date DATE, end_date DATE, interval_diff INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO date_test (id, start_date, end_date) VALUES (1, '2015-01-01', '2016-01-01');
UPDATE date_test SET interval_diff = end_date - start_date WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, DATE_DIFF(end_date, start_date) AS day_diff FROM date_test;
SELECT id, CAST(DATE_DIFF(end_date, start_date) AS DOUBLE) AS day_diff FROM date_test;
key_bug_features: Date difference calculation returns incorrect interval type
